subject.title: Agent draft: verify JWT signature on incoming webhooks

tldr: Agent draft. Adds a JWT signature check at the top of the webhook handler before any DB work. The shared secret comes from `process.env.WEBHOOK_SECRET`. Unsigned or mis-signed requests get a 401 with no body.

groups.title: Entry: webhook handler
groups.intro: Single hot path. The new guard runs before everything else in the handler, so anything past the check can keep assuming the request is signed.

lines.content: export const handleWebhook = async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
const event = await db.events.create({ payload: req.body })

annotations.title: Missing `WEBHOOK_SECRET` silently accepts a known HMAC
annotations.body: If `WEBHOOK_SECRET` is unset, `createHmac('sha256', '')` still produces a deterministic digest. A caller who knows the empty-secret HMAC of their payload could forge a request. Probably want to fail closed (throw on startup, or 503 here) instead of leaking that path.

annotations.title: Signature covers stringified body, not raw bytes
annotations.body: The verifier signs `JSON.stringify(req.body)` — whatever Express already parsed. Senders that include insignificant whitespace or key-ordering differences will fail signature, even when the parsed value matches. If we control both ends that's fine; flagging in case we don't.

suggestions.body: Refuse to start if the secret is missing, instead of accepting empty-secret HMACs at request time.

reviewPrompts: Is the empty-secret fallback acceptable, or should the process refuse to start without WEBHOOK_SECRET?
Does the signature scheme need to cover raw request bytes rather than the parsed body?
